seoTitle: How to Change Your Accountant on QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Update accountant access on QuickBooks Online so the right person has permissions when you switch accounting firms.
intro: Switching accountants or accounting firms means updating who has access to your QuickBooks Online account, which matters for both security and a smooth handoff. This guide covers navigating settings and updating accountant permissions. It's aimed at small business owners and accountants managing that transition.

steps.heading: Navigate to QuickBooks settings
steps.body: Access the account management area where user and accountant permissions are set.

steps.heading: Update accountant permissions
steps.body: Remove the previous accountant's access and invite the new one with appropriate permissions.

steps.heading: Review user access
steps.body: Confirm that only the intended people have access to sensitive financial data.

steps.heading: Apply final tips and best practices
steps.body: Double-check permission levels so the new accountant has exactly the access they need, no more or less.

faq.question: Does the previous accountant lose access immediately after removal?
faq.answer: Yes, removing their access should revoke it right away, though it's worth confirming after the change.

faq.question: Can I have more than one accountant with access at a time?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online can support multiple users with accountant-level access if needed during a transition.

faq.question: Will changing accountants affect my existing financial data?
faq.answer: No, your data remains intact — only who can access and manage it changes.
